page: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-192-metal is a compute-optimized bare metal server featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8481C processors. Operating at a clock speed of 4.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this dedicated hardware configuration includes 768 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz. The processor features a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It achieved a multi-core stress-ng score of 160,623.56 and a Passmark CPU score of 123,975.15. Memory performance is exceptionally strong, with read-only small block bandwidth reaching 13,143.3 GB/sec and large block write bandwidth at 202.24 GB/sec. Additionally, the server demonstrates top-tier efficiency in Redis SET operations, processing 182.38 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 140.64 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-standard-192-metal provides high resource density for compute-bound workloads, though it lacks local storage, requiring external storage solutions. This bare-metal instance is highly suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference, where its AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets deliver strong performance.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-192-metal is a compute-optimized bare metal server equipped with 192 vCPUs, 96 physical Intel Xeon 8481C cores running at 4.0 GHz, and 768 GB of 5 GHz DDR5 memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features dedicated CPU allocation with advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and superior database operations, highlighted by a Redis SET throughput of 182.38 million operations per second. While the server lacks local storage, its high resource density makes it highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy deployments. It is best suited for high-throughput web serving, enterprise database workloads, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-192-metal is a bare metal server with 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and database throughput.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-standard-192-metal is a bare metal server with 192 vCPUs and 768 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare metal server featuring 192 dedicated vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding compute and database workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated vCPUs and 96 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 4.0 GHz
768 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory with top-tier bandwidth
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top-tier performance in database operations and multi-core CPU benchmarks
No local storage, requiring external storage configurations
Optimized for enterprise databases, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
